title: Top medalists at Rio 2016
description: Top medalists from Rio 2016 based on the number of gold medals. The snippet uses the pivot type provier to aggregate the data and shows a simple bar chart with the results.
author: Tomas Petricek
code:
let data =
  olympics
    .'filter data'.'Games is'.'Rio (2016)'.then
    .'group data'.'by Athlete'.'sum Gold'.then
    .'sort data'.'by Gold descending'.then
    .paging.take(8)
    .'get series'.'with key Athlete'.'and value Gold'
  
chart.column(data).legend(position="none")
  .set(fontName="Roboto", fontSize=14)
  .set(colors=["#F4C300"], title="Top medalists (by number of gold medals) at Rio 2016")

title: Top medalists from London 2012
description: Top medalists from London 2012 games based on the number of gold medals. The snippet uses the pivot type provider to aggregate the data and shows a simple bar chart with the results.
author: Tomas Petricek
code:
let data =
  olympics
    .'filter data'.'Games is'.'London (2012)'.then
    .'group data'.'by Athlete'.'sum Gold'.then
    .'sort data'.'by Gold descending'.then
    .paging.take(8)
    .'get series'.'with key Athlete'.'and value Gold'
  
chart.column(data).legend(position="none")
  .set(fontName="Roboto", fontSize=14)
  .set(colors=["#50A3F4"], title="Top medalists (by number of gold medals) in London 2012")

title: Number of distinct countries in Olympics
description: Number of distinct countries competing in the Olympic games. The visualization shows that the number of distinct teams has been steadily growing since the beginning of the new Olympic games.
author: Tomas Petricek
code:
let data =
  olympics
    .'group data'.'by Games'.'average Year'.'count distinct Team'.then
    .'sort data'.'by Year'.then
    .'get series'.'with key Year'.'and value Team'

chart.area(data).legend(position="none")
  .set(fontName="Roboto", fontSize=14)
  .set(colors=["#6400C3"], title="Number of countries (teams) in Olympics")

title: Olympic medals table by athlete
description: Table showing top 10 athletes by the number of medals together with their team and discipline in which they compete. The sample creates a simple table and uses the pivot type provider for grouping.
author: Tomas Petricek
code:
let data = 
  olympics
    .'group data'.'by Athlete'.'sum Gold'.'sum Silver'.'sum Bronze'
       .'concatenate values of Discipline'.'concatenate values of Team'.then
    .'sort data'.'by Gold descending'.'and by Silver descending'
       .'and by Bronze descending'.then
    .paging.take(10)
    .'get the data'

table.create(data)

title: You Draw It: Carbon emissions of USA
description: Can you guess how CO2 emissions changed between 2005 and 2010 if you only see data up to 2005? Inspired by the NYT "You Draw It" series, this demo lets you draw your prediction directly on a line chart.
author: Tomas Petricek
code:
let ts = 
  worldbank.byCountry
    .'United States'.'Climate Change'
    .'CO2 emissions (kt)'.take(10)

youdraw.create(ts)
  .setRange(5000000, 6400000).setColors("#4060c0","#a040b0")

title: You Draw It: Olympic medals won by China
description: After going down from 76 to 53, the number of Olympic medals won by china started rising in 1988. Can you guess the rest of the trend, including the Olympic games in Beijing? (Inspired by NYT's piece mapping Obama's legacy and using the D3 code shared by authors).
author: Tomas Petricek
code:
let ts = 
  olympics.'filter data'.'Team is'.China.then
    .'group data'.'by Games'.'average Year'.'count all'.then
    .'sort data'.'by Year'.then
    .'get series'.'with key Year'.'and value count'

youdraw.create(ts)
  .setRange(0, 200).setColors("#ff5020","#808080").setClip(1996)

title: You Guess: Population of top 6 countries of the world
description: Inspired by the "You Draw It" visualisation from the New York Times, this demo lets you guess values in a bar chart. Can you correctly rank the 6 most populous countries in the world?
author: Tomas Petricek
code:
let data = 
  worldbank.byYear.'2012'.'All Indicators'.'Population, total'
    .sortValues(true).take(6).shuffle()

youguess.columns(data)
  .setLabel("Drag the bars and guess the population of 6 largest countries of the world!")

title: Top countries at Rio 2016
description: Top countries from Rio 2016 based on the number of gold medals. The snippet uses the pivot type provider to aggregate the data and shows a simple bar chart with the results.
author: Tomas Petricek
code:
let data =
  olympics
    .'filter data'.'Games is'.'Rio (2016)'.then
    .'group data'.'by Team'.'sum Gold'.then
    .'sort data'.'by Gold descending'.then
    .paging.take(8)
    .'get series'.'with key Team'.'and value Gold'
  
chart.column(data).legend(position="none")
  .set(fontName="Roboto", fontSize=14)
  .set(colors=["#F4C300"], title="Top medalists (by number of gold medals) at Rio 2016")

title: Guess the number of Olympic teams
description: This interactive visualization lets you guess the number of teams competing in the last 5 Olympic games. The initial values show the average number, so expect this to go down for some and up for others!
author: Tomas Petricek
code:
let data =
  olympics
    .'group data'.'by Year'.'count distinct Team'.'concatenate values of Games'.then
    .'sort data'.'by Year descending'.then
    .paging.take(5)
    .'sort data'.'by Year'.then  
    .'get series'.'with key Games'.'and value Team'

youguess.columns(data)

title: Guess the number of Turing people
description: The Alan Turing Institute hosts faculty fellows, research fellows, and PhD students from partner universities at its London offices. Can you guess how many people work in each category?
author: Tomas Petricek

title: Guess the number of Turing people (2.)
description: The Alan Turing Institute hosts a number of faculty fellows visiting from partner universities, research fellows and PhD students working at the London offices and a also welcomes visiting researchers. Guess how many of each are there! This version uses data from the turing.ac.uk website.
author: Tomas Petricek
code:
let data =
  shared.'by date'.'May 2017'.'The Alan Turing Institute People (May 2017)'
    .'group data'.'by Role'.'count all'.then
    .'get series'.'with key Role'.'and value count'

youguess.columns(data)

title: Guess the number of Turing people (3.)
description: The Alan Turing Institute hosts a number of faculty fellows visiting from partner universities, research fellows and PhD students working at the London offices and a also welcomes visiting researchers. Guess how many of each are there! This version uses data from the turing.ac.uk website. (Corrected!)
author: Tomas Petricek
code:
let data =
  shared.'by date'.'May 2017'.'The Alan Turing Institute People (May 2017, corrected)'
    .'group data'.'by Role'.'count all'.then
    .'get series'.'with key Role'.'and value count'

youguess.columns(data)

title: Cambridge Faculty Fellows
description: A table showing Cambridge Faculty Fellows at the Alan Turing Institute, using data scraped from the turing.ac.uk website.
author: Tomas Petricek
code:
let data =
  shared.'by date'.'May 2017'.'The Alan Turing Institute People (7 May 2017)'
    .'filter data'.'Position is'.'Faculty Fellow'.'and University is'.Cambridge.then
    .'get the data'

table.create(data)

title: New Rio 2016 Golds
description: Testing a new version of just the Rio data, which gives the right number of gold medals
author: Tom Knowles
code:
let data =
  shared.'by date'.'May 2017'.'Rio 2016 Olympics'
    .'filter data'.'Games is'.'Rio (2016)'.then
    .'group data'.'by Event'.'and Discipline'.'and Team'.'and Gold'.then
    .'group data'.'by Team'.'sum Gold'.then
    .'sort data'.'by Gold descending'.then
    .paging.take(8)
    .'get series'.'with key Team'.'and value Gold'

chart.column(data).legend(position="none")
  .set(fontName="Roboto,sans-serif", fontSize=14)
  .set(colors=["#F4C300"], title="Top countries at Rio 2016 by the number of Gold medals")

title: Top 10 Government Departments by 2015-16 Expenditure
description: A demo of the UK Public Expenditure Statistical Analyses data showing the top 10 government departments by spending in 2015-16, with a breakdown of who is spending your money on what.
author: Tom Knowles
code:
let data =
  shared.'by date'.'May 2017'.'Central government own expenditure in budgets by departmental group (PESA 2016)'.'group data'.'by Department'.'sum 2015-16 Outturn'.then
    .'sort data'.'by 2015-16 Outturn descending'.then
    .paging.take(10)
    .'get series'.'with key Department'.'and value 2015-16 Outturn'
    
chart.column(data).legend(position="none")
  .set(colors=["Pink"])
  .set(title="The top 10 highest-spending government departmental groups in 2015-16")
  .vAxis(title="Expenditure in GBP millions")

title: Most viewed Turing videos on YouTube
description: Top 10 videos from YouTube based on the number of views. This is based on the number of people who watched at least a bit of the video.
author: Tomas Petricek
code:
let data =
  shared.'by date'.'May 2017'.'YouTube views by video'
    .'sort data'.'by Views descending'.then
    .paging.take(10)
    .'get series'.'with key Video title'.'and value Views'

chart.bar(data)
  .set(title="Enter chart title", colors=["#77aae0"])
  .set(fontName="Roboto", fontSize=13)
  .legend(position="none")

title: UK spending on tertiary education
description: A minimal demo using the UK expenditure data service
author: Tomas Petricek

title: UK spending on tertiary education
description: A minimal demo using the UK expenditure data service. Less misleading version with Y axis starting from zero.
author: Tomas Petricek

title: You Draw It: Carbon emissions of USA (new)
description: Can you guess how the CO2 emissions have been changing between 2005 and 2010 if you see data between 2000 and 2005? Inspired by the fantastic "You Draw It: What Got Better or Worse During Obama?s Presidency" piece in NYT (see link). New version using The Gama composable visualizations library.
author: Tomas Petricek
code:
let ts = 
  worldbank.byCountry
    .'United States'.'Climate Change'
    .'CO2 emissions (kt)'.take(10).sortKeys()

youguess.line(ts)
  .setRange(5000000, 6400000).setColors("#4060c0","#a040b0")

title: GDP and Mark Spitz' medals
description: This is a demo showing how to add markers to a youguess line chart. It shows US GDP per capita with the number of gold medals won by Mark Spitz. As you can see, there is a very strong connection between the two... not.
author: Tomas Petricek
code:
let data =
  worldbank.byCountry.'United States'.'Economy & Growth'
    .'GDP per capita (current US$)'

let markers =
   olympics
      .'filter data'.'Team is'.'United States'.'and Athlete is'.'Mark Spitz'.then
     .'group data'.'by Year'.'sum Gold'.then
     .'get series'.'with key Year'.'and value Gold'

youguess.line(data).setMarkers(markers)
   .setColors("blue","green")
   .setMarkerColor("red")
   .setLabels("US GDP per capita and Mark Spitz' medals","","")

title: Video views per date
description: YouTube video views per date from the Alan Turing Institute YouTube channel.
author: Tomas Petricek
code:
let data =
  shared.'by date'.'May 2017'.'YouTube Views per Date'
    .'get series'.'with key date'.'and value views'

chart.column(data)
  .set(title="Viedo views per date", colors=["#77aae0"])
  .set(fontName="Roboto", fontSize=13)
  .legend(position="none")

title: Classification of the Functions of Government (COFOG)
description: COFOG classifies government expenditure data by the purpose for which funds are used. It is developed by the Organisation for Economic Co-operation and Development (OECD).
author: May Yong
code:
let data =
  shared.'by date'.'May 2017'.'COFOG Structure'
    .'filter data'.'Service is'.'Social protection'.then.'get the data'

table.create(data)

title: Compost: CO2 emissions line chart
description: Simple visalization showing the CO2 emissions of China and USA using a new experimental compost library.
author: Tomas Petricek
code:
let l1 = 
  worldbank.byCountry.'United States'.'Climate Change'
    .'CO2 emissions (kt)'
let l2 = 
  worldbank.byCountry.China.'Climate Change'
    .'CO2 emissions (kt)'    

compost.charts.lines([l1,l2])
  .setAxisX(1960, 2010)
  .setAxisY(label="CO2 emissions (kt)")
  .setColors(["blue","red"])

title: Compost: Top polluters bar chart
description: Simple visualization showing top 8 polluters based on CO2 emissions. This illustrates new experimental charting library compost.
author: Tomas Petricek
code:
let co2 = 
  worldbank.byYear.'2010'.'Climate Change'
    .'CO2 emissions (kt)'
    .sortValues(reverse=true).take(8)  
    
compost.charts.column(co2)  
  .setAxisX(label="Countries")
  .setAxisY(label="CO2 emissions (kt)")

title: Unemployment in Italy
description: Male and female unemployment in Italy in percentage of labor force since 80's
author: Gianluca Bigaglia
code:
let maleData =
  worldbank
    .byCountry.Italy.Education.'Unemployment, male (% of male labor force)'

let femaleData =
  worldbank
    .byCountry.Italy.Education.'Unemployment, female (% of female labor force)'

table.create(femaleData)

compost.charts.lines([maleData, femaleData])
  .setAxisY(0, 18, "Unemployment in Italy in (% of labor force)")
  .setAxisX(1980,2010,"Blue: Male, Red: Female")
  .setColors(["blue","red"])

title: You Draw It: United Kingdom CO2 emissions with axis labels
description: This example shows how to add axis labels to youdraw.line chart. Some of the other options are not yet supported, but labels work as expected!
author: Tomas Petricek
code:
let uk = 
  worldbank.byCountry.'United Kingdom'.'Climate Change'
    .'CO2 emissions (kt)'
      
youguess.line(uk)
  .setAxisX(label="Years")
  .setAxisY(label="CO2 emissions (kt)")

title: Guess the number of Olympic teams
description: Guess the number of teams in Olympic games, using a locally uploaded version of the medal data.
author: Tomas Petricek
code:
let data =
  shared.'by date'.'June 2017'.'Olympic medalists'
    .'group data'.'by Year'.'count distinct Team'.then
    .'sort data'.'by Year'.then
    .'get series'.'with key Year'.'and value Team'

youguess.line(data)
  .setColors("#f0b030", "#f0b030")
  .setTitle("Number of distinct teams in the Olympics")
  .setDrawColor("gray")
  .setClip(1960)

title: John vs. James in Olympic games
description: How many athletes named John or James competed in the Olympic games throughout history? Explore the popularity of these names across different eras using the full Olympic medals dataset.
author: Tomas Petricek
code:
let james = 
  shared.'by date'.'June 2017'.'Olympic medalists'.'filter data'.'Athlete contains'("James").then
  .'group data'.'by Year'.'count distinct Athlete'.then
  .windowing.'window by Year'(5).'middle Year'.'mean Athlete'.then
  .'sort data'.'by middle Year'.then
  .'get series'.'with key middle Year'.'and value Athlete'
  .setProperties(seriesName="James")

let john = 
  shared.'by date'.'June 2017'.'Olympic medalists'.'filter data'.'Athlete contains'("John").then
  .'group data'.'by Year'.'count distinct Athlete'.then
  .windowing.'window by Year'(5).'middle Year'.'mean Athlete'.then
  .'sort data'.'by middle Year'.then
  .'get series'.'with key middle Year'.'and value Athlete'
  .setProperties(seriesName="John")

compost.charts.lines([james,john])
  .setAxisX(minValue=1904, maxValue=2008)
  .setLegend("right")

title: F# Survey 2017: How you use F#?
description: The snippet creates an interactive chart visualizing the answer to "How would you characterize your use of F# today?" from the F# Survey 2017. The snippet uses a new way of getting type provider for live CSV file using URL.
author: Tomas Petricek
code:
let fsharp = 
  web.load("https://raw.githubusercontent.com/the-gamma/workyard/master/fsharp/fsharp-survey-2017.csv").explore
    .'group data'.'by How would you characterize your use of F# today?'.'count all'.then
    .'sort data'.'by count descending'.then
    .paging.take(5).'get series'.'with key How would you characterize your use of F# today?'.'and value count'

youguess.bars(fsharp)

title: F# Survey 2017: How you use F#?
description: The snippet creates an interactive chart visualizing the answer to "How would you characterize your use of F# today?" from the F# Survey  2017. The snippet uses a new way of getting type provider for live CSV file using URL. (Less obvious version with shuffle added!)
author: Tomas Petricek
code:
let fsharp = 
  web.load("https://raw.githubusercontent.com/the-gamma/workyard/master/fsharp/fsharp-survey-2017.csv").explore
    .'group data'.'by How would you characterize your use of F# today?'.'count all'.then
    .'sort data'.'by count descending'.then
    .paging.take(5).'get series'.'with key How would you characterize your use of F# today?'.'and value count'

youguess.bars(fsharp.shuffle())

title: Cambridge and Oxford science events in 2016
description: The snippet extracts data from a Wikipedia page of important events in science in 2016 and shows them. You can see the source code and verify how the data flows from the Wikipedia page to the resulting table.
author: Tomas Petricek
code:
let events = 
  web.scrape("https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2016_in_science").explore
    .'filter data'.'Heading 2 is'.'Events[edit]'.then
    .'filter data'.'Item contains'("Oxford").'or Item contains'("Cambridge").then
    .'drop columns'.'drop Heading 2'.then
    .'get the data'
    
table.create(events)

title: Turing Event visits
description: The visualization shows total number of event visits at the Turing Institute - this counts people separately per event, so if you went to two events, you will be counted twice.
author: Tomas Petricek
code:
let data =
  shared.'by date'.'July 2017'.'Turing Events (3 July 2017)'
    .windowing.'expanding by Date'.'sum Number'.then
    .'get series'.'with key last Date'.'and value Number'

compost.charts.area(data)
  .setColors(["#ff7f0e"])
  .setAxisX(minValue=date.date(2015,10,10), maxValue=date.date(2017,4,1))
  .setAxisY(label="Total number of event visits")
  .setSize(height=300)

title: Number of attendees per kind of event
description: This visualization shows the total number of attendees per different kind of events held at the Alan Turing Institute.
author: Tomas Petricek
code:
let data =
  shared.'by date'.'July 2017'.'Turing Events (3 July 2017, corrected)'
    .'filter data'.'Kind is not'.Masterclass.'and Kind is not'.'External event'.then
    .'group data'.'by Kind'.'sum Number'.then
    .'get series'.'with key Kind'.'and value Number'

compost.charts.bar(data)
  .setAxisY(label="Kind of event", labelOffset=100)

title: Tomas' papers at the Turing
description: This just tests basic search in pivot type provider and also uploads the list of publications scraped from Turing website

code:
let data =
  shared.'by date'.'July 2017'.'Turing Papers (3 July 2017, raw)'
    .'filter data'.'Details contains'("Petricek").then
    .'get the data'

table.create(data)

title: FTSE100: Moving average
description: Displays moving average of the FTSE closing price over a very long window.
author: Tomas Petricek
code:
let data =
  shared.'by date'.'August 2017'.'FTSE100 (2012-2017)'
    .windowing.'window by Date'(50).'last Date'.'mean Open'.'mean Close'.then
    .'get series'.'with key last Date'.'and value Close'

compost.charts.line(data)
 .setAxisX(minValue=date.date(2012,10,12), maxValue=date.date(2017,8,1))

title: Mortality Rate in USA
description: Male and female mortality rate since 1980
author: Tobias
code:
let maleData =
  worldbank
    .byCountry.'United States'.Health.'Mortality rate, adult, male (per 1,000 male adults)'
    .map(fun x -> x / 1000)

let femaleData =
  worldbank
    .byCountry.'United States'.Health.'Mortality rate, adult, female (per 1,000 female adults)'
    .map(fun x -> x / 1000)

table.create(femaleData)

compost.charts.lines([maleData, femaleData])
  .setAxisY(0, 2, "Mortality Rate USA (in %)")
  .setAxisX(1980,2010,"Blue: Male, Red: Female")
  .setColors(["blue","red"])

title: MozFest - Government expenditure
description: Sample visualization showing top 7 services from the UK government expenditure. For more examples, see: http://gamma.turing.ac.uk/expenditure/
author: Tomas Petricek
code:
let data = 
  expenditure.byYear.'2015'.inTermsOf.GDP
    .sortValues(reverse=true).skip(1).take(7)

compost.charts.bar(data)
  .setAxisY(label="", labelOffset=150)

title: MozFest - University enrolment
description: Sample visualization using the World Bank type provider to compare university enrolment across several European countries.
author: Tomas Petricek
code:
let fr = 
  worldbank.byCountry.France.Education
    .'Enrolment in tertiary education per 100,000 inhabitants, both sexes'
    .setProperties(seriesName="France")

let dk = 
  worldbank.byCountry.Denmark.Education
    .'Enrolment in tertiary education per 100,000 inhabitants, both sexes'
    .setProperties(seriesName="Denmark")
 
let uk = 
  worldbank.byCountry.'United Kingdom'.Education
    .'Enrolment in tertiary education per 100,000 inhabitants, both sexes'
    .setProperties(seriesName="United Kingdom")

compost.charts.lines([fr, uk, dk])
  .setAxisX(minValue=1971, maxValue=2011)
  .setAxisY(minValue=0)
  .setLegend(position="auto")
  .setTitle("Enrolment in tertiary education")

title: MozFest - Number of Olympic teams
description: Sample visualization using the pivot type provider to aggregate data on Olympic games. It shows number of distinct teams per year.
author: Tomas Petricek
code:
let teams = 
  olympics
    .'group data'.'by Year'.'count distinct Team'.then
    .'sort data'.'by Year'.then
    .'get series'.'with key Year'.'and value Team'

compost.charts.line(teams)
  .setAxisX(minValue=1896, maxValue=2020)
  .setTitle("Number of disticnt teams in Olympics")

title: MozFest - Turing fellows
description: Sample visualization that uses custom CSV file with information about Turing people. It counts the number of Turing Fellows by University.
author: Tomas Petricek
code:

  shared.'by date'.'July 2017'.'Turing People (23 July)'
    .'filter data'.'Position is'.'Turing Fellow'.then
    .'group data'.'by University'.'count all'.then
    .'get series'.'with key University'.'and value count'

compost.charts.column(fellows)
  .setTitle("Turing Fellows by University")

title: MozFest - Guess US emissions
description: Sample visualization that asks the reader to make a guess. In this demo, we use WorldBank data to let the user guess CO2 emissions of of the US
author: Tomas Petricek
code:
let co2 = 
  worldbank.byCountry.'United States'.'Climate Change'
    .'CO2 emissions (metric tons per capita)'

youguess.line(co2)
   .setClip(1985)
   .setAxisY(maxValue=35)
   .setColors("#309040", "#304090")
   .setTitle("United States - CO2 emissions")

title: MozFest - Guess number of gold medals
description: Sample visualization showing an interactive chart where you have to make a guess first. Here, guess the total number of Gold medals per athlete.
author: Tomas Petricek
code:
let athletes = 
  olympics.'group data'.'by Athlete'.'sum Gold'.then
    .'sort data'.'by Gold descending'.then
    .paging.take(6)
    .'get series'.'with key Athlete'.'and value Gold'

youguess.bars(athletes)
  .setLabel("Guess the number of gold medals")

title: MozFest - University enrolment
description:
Sample visualization using the World Bank type provider to compare university enrolment across several European countries.
(now with Brazil!)
author: Marcos
code:
let fr = 
  worldbank.byCountry.France.Education
    .'Enrolment in tertiary education per 100,000 inhabitants, both sexes'
    .setProperties(seriesName="France")

let dk = 
  worldbank.byCountry.Denmark.Education
    .'Enrolment in tertiary education per 100,000 inhabitants, both sexes'
    .setProperties(seriesName="Denmark")
 
let uk = 
  worldbank.byCountry.'United Kingdom'.Education
    .'Enrolment in tertiary education per 100,000 inhabitants, both sexes'
    .setProperties(seriesName="United Kingdom")

let br =
  worldbank.byCountry.Brazil.Education
  .'Enrolment in tertiary education per 100,000 inhabitants, both sexes'
  .setProperties(seriesName="Brazil")

compost.charts.lines([fr, uk, dk, br])
  .setAxisX(minValue=1971, maxValue=2005)
  .setAxisY(minValue=0)
  .setLegend(position="auto")
  .setTitle("Enrolment in tertiary education")

title: MozFest - Trees in Linz, Austria
description: Number of Trees grouped by type
author: Bernhard
code:
let data =
  shared.'by date'.'October 2017'.'Trees in the City of Linz, Austria'
    .'drop columns'.then
    .'group data'.'by Art'.'average Flaeche'.'average Hoehe'.'count all'.then
    .'get series'.'with key Art'.'and value count'

chart.bar(data)

title: Libraries
description: Library counts by library service
author: Data Treaders
code:
let data =
  shared.'by date'.'October 2017'.'Library counts by library service'.'group data'
  .'by Library service'
  .'count distinct Library name'
  .then.'sort data'.'by Library name descending'
  .then.paging.take(20)
  .'get series'.'with key Library service'.'and value Library name'

chart.pie(data)

title: test
description: test
author: Ezequiel Paura

  uploaded
    .'group data'.'by p_pais'.'count all'.'average p_estado'.then
    .'get series'.'with key p_pais'.'and value count'

title: Gender Pay Gap
description: Percentage difference in mean hourly pay between genders.
author: proteinsAreDifficult
code:
let data =
  shared.'by date'.'April 2018'.'Gender pay gap'
    .'drop columns'.'drop Address'.'drop CompanyLinkToGPGInfo'.'drop CompanyNumber'.'drop SicCodes'.then
    .'sort data'.'by DiffMeanHourlyPercent descending'.then
    .'get series'.'with key CurrentName'.'and value DiffMeanHourlyPercent'.take(5).shuffle()

youguess.bars(data)
   .setLabel("Drag the bars, guess the hourly mean percentage difference between gender pay.")

    .'group data'.'by Team'.'sum Gold'.then
    .'sort data'.'by Gold descending'.then
    .paging.take(10)
    .'get series'.'with key Team'.'and value Gold'

chart.bar(data)

title: Trade war between US and China
description: Guess how much does US import from China and vice versa and how large proportion of this will be affected by tariffs.

  shared.'by date'.'July 2018'.'US China trade war'
    .'get series'.'with key Information'.'and value Value'
    .reverse()

youguess.bars(data)

code:

    .'get series'.'with key Category'.'and value Total'

youguess.bars(data).setAxisX(label="Imports from USA to China in 2016 (billions of $)")

title: Trade war between US and China (with legend)
description: Guess how much does US import from China and vice versa and how large proportion of this will be affected by tariffs.
author: Tomas Petricek
code:
let data =
  shared.'by date'.'August 2018'.'Trade war between US and China'
    .'get series'.'with key Information'.'and value Value'
    .reverse()

youguess.bars(data).setAxisX(maxValue=500, label="Total exports and exports affected by tariffs (in billions of US dollars)")

title: Guess UK government expenditure
description: Following the Classification of the Functions of Government, we split the overall expenditure into ten services. Can you guess how much we spent (as a percentage of GDP) on the top six UK public services?
author: Tomas Petricek
code:
let data =
  expenditure
    .byYear.'2015'
    .inTermsOf.GDP
    .sortValues(reverse=true).skip(1).take(5).shuffle()

youguess.bars(data)
  .setTitle("Expenditure in % of GDP (2015)")
  .setAxisY(label="", labelOffset=110, labelMinimalSize=500)

title: US v China
description: US v China co2 Needs work
author: heckvo
code:
let data =
  worldbank
    .byCountry.'United States'.'Climate Change'
    .'CO2 emissions (kt)'

let data2 =
  worldbank
    .byCountry.'China'.'Climate Change'
    .'CO2 emissions (kt)'

chart.line(data.setProperties(seriesName="US").append(data2.setProperties(seriesName="China")))
  .set(title="co2", colors=["#5588e0","#ff0000"])
  .set(fontName="Roboto", fontSize=13)
  .legend(position="bottom")

title: Kent Lords travel expenses
description: Top 3 members of the House of Lords from Kent by the amount spent on travel expneses.
author: Tomas Petricek
code:
let data =
  shared.'by date'.'March 2020'.'Lords Travel Expenses'
    .'filter data'.'County is'.Kent.then
    .'sort data'.'by Travel Costs descending'.then
    .paging.take(3)
    .'get series'.'with key Name'.'and value Travel Costs'

compost.charts.column(data)

title: If Phelps was a country
description: Michael Phelps has more Gold medals from the Olympic games than many countries.
author: Tomas Petricek
code:
let phelps =
  olympics.'filter data'.'Athlete is'.'Michael Phelps'.then
    .'group data'.'by Athlete'.'sum Gold'.then
    .'get series'.'with key Athlete'.'and value Gold'

let data =
  olympics
    .'group data'.'by Team'.'sum Gold'.then
    .'sort data'.'by Gold descending'.then
    .paging.skip(42).take(6)
    .'get series'.'with key Team'.'and value Gold'

compost.charts.bar(data.append(phelps).sortValues(true))
  .setColors(["red","red","red","blue"])
  .setAxisY(label="yo", labelOffset=200)

title: If Phelps was a country (nicer colors)
description: Michael Phelps has won more Gold medals than many entire countries have earned throughout Olympic history. This chart puts his extraordinary achievement in perspective using the full medals dataset.
author: Tomas Petricek
code:
let phelps =
  olympics.'filter data'.'Athlete is'.'Michael Phelps'.then
    .'group data'.'by Athlete'.'sum Gold'.then
    .'get series'.'with key Athlete'.'and value Gold'

let data =
  olympics
    .'group data'.'by Team'.'sum Gold'.then
    .'sort data'.'by Gold descending'.then
    .paging.skip(42).take(6)
    .'get series'.'with key Team'.'and value Gold'

compost.charts.bar(data.append(phelps).sortValues(true))
  .setColors(["#aec7e8","#aec7e8","#aec7e8","#1f77b4"])
  .setAxisY(label="", labelOffset=200)
  .setTitle("If Michael Phelps was a country...")
  .setSize(700,350)

title: Who does the Doctor fight most frequently?
description: Using a graph database of Doctor Who episodes, this demo finds the most frequent enemies faced by The Doctor. Which villain has appeared most often throughout the show's long history?
author: Tomas Petricek
code:
let topEnemies =
  drWho.Character.Doctor
    .'ENEMY_OF'.'[any]'.'APPEARED_IN'.'[any]'.'explore_properties'
  .explore
    .'group data'.'by 1-name'.'count distinct 2-name'.then
    .'sort data'.'by 2-name descending'.then
    .paging.take(7).'get series'.'with key 1-name'.'and value 2-name'

compost.charts.bar(topEnemies.reverse())
  .setAxisX(maxValue=40)
  .setTitle("Who does the Doctor fight most frequently?")

title: COVID-19 cases in London on 5 October 2020
description: London boroughs by the total number of new infections on 5 October, directly using the official data from data.london.gov.uk
author: Tomas Petricek
code:
let data = 
 web.loadTable("https://data.london.gov.uk/download/coronavirus--covid-19--cases/151e497c-a16e-414e-9e03-9e428f555ae9/phe_cases_london_boroughs.csv")
  .explore.'filter data'.'date is in range'(date.date(2020,10,5), date.date(2020,10,6)).then
  .'sort data'.'by new_cases descending'.then
  .paging.take(15)
  .'get series'.'with key area_name'.'and value new_cases'
  .reverse()

compost.charts.bar(data).setAxisY(label="", labelOffset=150)

title: COVID-19 cases in Camden and Richmond
description: Comparing daily new COVID-19 cases in Camden and Richmond over time, using official data from data.london.gov.uk. The two London boroughs follow surprisingly similar patterns throughout the pandemic.
author: Tomas Petricek

title: CO2 emissions -- USA vs China
description: CO2 emissions of China and USA
author: Layus
code:
let usa = worldbank.byCountry.'United States'.'Climate Change'.'CO2 emissions (kt)'
let china = worldbank.byCountry.'China'.'Climate Change'.'CO2 emissions (kt)'

let datas = series.values([usa.setProperties("","","USA"), china.setProperties("","","China")])

chart.lines(datas)
    .hAxis(format="#", title = "Year")
    .vAxis(logScale = true, format = ",###", title = "CO2 emissions (kt)")
